page: Amazon Web Services g4ad.8xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores running at 3.0 GHz. The system features 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. For graphics and parallel computing acceleration, the instance is equipped with two AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPUs delivering a total of 16 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided via a 1200 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and network connectivity is supported by a 15 Gbps baseline bandwidth under the Nitro hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate a balanced profile with notable strengths in specific areas. The instance demonstrates strong performance in Passmark CPU encryption and Passmark memory tests, placing in the top 25%. Multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and software compilation with Clang fall within the average range. However, single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, scoring in the bottom 10% on stress-ng single-core tests. Large-scale LLM inference tasks, such as prompt processing for a 70B model, also show weaker performance, though smaller 135M and 7B models achieve average throughput.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g4ad.8xlarge offers a balanced resource density by bundling dedicated AMD EPYC CPU cores, AMD Radeon Pro GPUs, and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage. This hardware combination makes it suitable for graphics-intensive workloads, game streaming, video transcoding, and moderate machine learning inference. It is also well-suited for database operations and general-purpose applications that benefit from local NVMe storage and GPU acceleration without requiring top-tier single-core CPU performance.
description: Amazon Web Services g4ad.8xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 32 vCPUs, 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and two AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPUs with 16 GB of total VRAM. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes a 1200 GB local NVMe SSD and 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The dedicated x86_64 architecture supports AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instructions, ensuring efficient cryptographic operations. Benchmark results show strong performance in CPU encryption and memory tests, while single-core CPU performance and large-scale LLM inference are weaker. The instance offers a balanced hardware profile for graphics rendering, video transcoding, and database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g4ad.8xlarge is a graphics-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs AMD EPYC 7R32, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, two AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPUs, and a 1200 GB NVMe SSD.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g4ad.8xlarge features 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, two AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPUs, and 1200 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A graphics-accelerated instance featuring dual AMD Radeon Pro GPUs, dedicated EPYC processors, and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 32 vCPUs and 128 GB DDR4 RAM
Equipped with two AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPUs providing 16 GB total VRAM
Includes 1200 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
Delivers 15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong performance in CPU encryption and memory benchmarks
Optimized for graphics rendering, video transcoding, and database workloads
